videos.title: Cycling in the Pyrenees - a cycle travel vlog: Cols! More Cols! Enough Cols!
videos.description:
Contact Dom and Liz at Pyrenees Pursuits on www.pyreneespursuits.com
Full disclosure:  We paid full price for our stay - no freebies!

Currently, revenue from my channel is donated to www.dec.org.uk to support humanitarian relief in Ukraine. 

My videos raise money for The Vine Food Bank in Croydon, UK. If you would like to make a separate donation, you may do so here:
https://www.justgiving.com/fundraising/newaddingtonsalvationarmy

Get your Nobody Cares apparel here:
https://teespring.com/en-GB/stores/julianhutchings
More items coming soon!

Making videos takes time, effort and a bit of money - if you fancy supporting the channel and helping to keep my creative juices flowing, you can buy me a virtual coffee here: https://www.buymeacoffee.com/somebodycares

If you want to see some of my still photos - go to my Instagram page.

I'm also on Facebook although I rarely post anything because it gets on my nerves. 

Amazon Affiliate Links - if you buy through these links, I get a small commission which goes to charity.

Photography equipment I use and recommend:
Panasonic GH5 (unboxing video available): https://amzn.to/2XFAhkK
Panasonic Lumix 7-14mm lens: https://amzn.to/2LRqHZe
Panasonic Lumix 12-35mm lens: https://amzn.to/3ia4dPq
Canon G7X Mkll: https://amzn.to/3nIguf1
Sony ZV1: https://amzn.to/2XJ6do8
Ulanzi wide angle lens adapter: https://amzn.to/3jzdpxZ
Small Rig cage for Sony ZV1: https://amzn.to/2Yjhn3i
GoPro Hero 7 Black: https://amzn.to/3oXojz7 
Ulanzi GoPro case: https://amzn.to/3qliWtM 
Pro Grill Mouth Mount for GoPro (Unboxing video available): https://amzn.to/38KJy1c
Rode Video Microphone: https://amzn.to/38Hi24z
Saramonic microphone: https://amzn.to/38JkNm3
Ulanzi mini foldable tripod: https://amzn.to/3CtWFRd
Neewer compact tripod: https://amzn.to/3nM9MER
Neewer full size tripod: https://amzn.to/38LvowZ
Neewer ring light (Unboxing video available): https://amzn.to/3speX0Y

Cycling stuff I use and recommend:
Exposure TraceR rear light: https://amzn.to/2N67aoI
See Sense rear light (unboxing video available): https://amzn.to/3oMeNOV
Tyrekey: https://amzn.to/3iek7YU
Vittoria tool bottle: https://amzn.to/3sp5FC8
Lezyne Road Caddy (video available): https://amzn.to/3oIoY7c
Kask Protone helmet: https://amzn.to/35F9cCL
Fizik Terra shoes: https://amzn.to/2LQqbuD
Shimano XC5 shoes (unboxing video available): https://amzn.to/2Km31fo
Speedsleev Ranger (unboxing video available): https://amzn.to/3idKf6t
High 5 hydration tablets: https://amzn.to/3qyvNcf

Books written by me!:
Cycling Shorts and Cycle Clips: https://amzn.to/2LSTQmN
Tour de Fiction: https://amzn.to/35FMY3u
The Monument: https://amzn.to/3qm8rGm
The Marmot Murders: https://amzn.to/3oLB2Vp
Unreliable Death: https://amzn.to/3bzxa66
Love's Lost in Girona:https://amzn.to/38IuxNg

Books I recommend:
Riding in the Zone Rouge by Tom Isitt (video review available): https://amzn.to/3iekH96
Ventoux by Bert Wagendorp: https://amzn.to/38HYnBv
Higher Calling by Max Leonard: https://amzn.to/3oLOskf
The Greatest: The Times and Life of Beryl Burton by William Fotheringham (video review available): https://amzn.to/3nFHADH
Fallen Angel: The Passion of Fausto Coppi by William Fotheringham: https://amzn.to/3nL8F8v
Lanterne Rouge by Max Leonard: https://amzn.to/3qBQrZ1
The Escape Artist by Matt Seaton: https://amzn.to/2LtsnZr
End to End by Paul Jones: https://amzn.to/3i518lb

Cycling movies I recommend:
Breaking Away: https://amzn.to/35QpF7a
A Sunday in Hell: https://amzn.to/38V7ak0
Pantani: The Accidental Death of a Cyclist: https://amzn.to/3bRlSdw

Follow me on Strava: https://www.strava.com/athletes/7528

key_challenges.name: Col du Peyresourde
key_challenges.description: A Tour de France climb where riders mention crashes occurred, notably where Pogacar crashed and held hands with Vingegaard during descent, indicating technical or dangerous descent conditions.

additional_quotes.text: yesterday was our first day of cycling and it was very thick thick mist very thick cloud and it was raining not all the time but most of the time

rider_intel.search_text: Limited race-specific information available from this transcript, which primarily focuses on accommodation at Pyrenees Pursuits rather than race conditions. The rider mentions experiencing thick mist, heavy cloud cover, and intermittent rain during their first day of cycling in the Pyrenees. They reference the Col du Peyresourde as a destination, noting it as a Tour de France climb where crashes have occurred, including a notable incident involving Pogacar and Vingegaard. Weather conditions appear variable with foggy, misty conditions in the mountains but clearing skies possible. The transcript suggests riders should be prepared for changing mountain weather patterns during Pyrenees cycling.
